merged_events.event_description: The United States established a consulate in Dhaka (then Dacca), which was at the time the eastern part of Pakistan.

evidence.quote: The United States established a consulate in Dhaka (then referred to as "Dacca") on August 29, 1949, when what is now called "Bangladesh" was the eastern part of Pakistan.

merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations and the American Embassy at Dhaka were established, with Herbert D. Spivack as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations and the American Embassy at Dhaka were established on May 18, 1972, with Herbert D. Spivack as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Embassy in Bangladesh established, with Herbert Daniel Spivack serving as the first Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

evidence.quote: Herbert Daniel Spivack, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 1972-05. Left post: 1972-10.

matched.notes: Exact date and status match. RDCR confirms U.S. consulate established in Dhaka on August 29, 1949.
Exact date and status match with RDCR (Event 1, 1972-05-18). POCOM (Event 2) gives the same event with month-level precision (1972-05), consistent with the RDCR date.
